channel.title: NYT > Arts
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: How the D4vd Case and Rumors Captivated the Public Before the Singer’s Arrest
item.description: A teenage girl went missing. The police found her remains in a musician’s car. Then the Los Angeles media machine got to work.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens
item.media:credit: Gina Ferazzi/Los Angeles Times, via Getty Images
item.media:description: A memorial for Celeste Rivas Hernandez, whose remains were found in a car trunk in September.

item.title: How TV Series Like ‘Smash’ and ‘American Classic’ Portray Theater Is Quite a Drama
item.description: As portrayed in shows like “Smash,” “Slings & Arrows” and “American Classic,” life onstage is a grab-bag of archetypes both hilariously wrong and a little bit right.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Green
item.category: Only Murders in the Building (TV Program)

item.title: Through A.I. Glasses-Powered Translation, Korea’s Theaters Hope for a K-Pop Moment
item.description: Producers and the cultural authorities hope that technology can overcome a language barrier and take the country’s shows to the world.
item.dc:creator: Hahna Yoon
item.media:credit: Jun Michael Park for The New York Times

item.title: ‘Proof’ Review: Ayo Edebiri as a Math Girl, Interrupted
item.description: The actress stars as a haunted genius opposite Don Cheadle as her father in David Auburn’s 2001 drama. This revival, though, exposes the play’s lack of rigor.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw
item.media:credit: Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
item.media:description: Don Cheadle, left, and Ayo Edebiri as father and daughter in the play “Proof” at the Booth Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: ‘The Pitt’ Season 2 Finale Recap: Jagged Little Pills
item.description: For all the death and darkness the Pitt crew had to swallow this season, the finale also offered profound moments of hope and renewal.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins
item.media:credit: HBO Max
item.media:description: Dana (Katherine LaNasa) hoped someone on staff would foster Baby Jane Doe. Has she found a taker?

item.title: ‘Lee Cronin’s The Mummy’ Is Out. Wait, Who’s Lee Cronin?
item.description: The latest take on Universal’s “Mummy” franchise has a director you may not know. We explain who he is, why his name is in the movie title and what he brings to the “Mummy” world.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Aguilar
item.media:credit: Warner Bros. Pictures
item.media:description: Veronica Falcón in a scene from “Lee Cronin’s The Mummy.”

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo’s ‘Drop Dead’ Is a Heavenly Fakeout
item.description: The first taste of the pop star’s third album isn’t a bloodthirsty kiss-off, but a dreamy ode to losing herself in love.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Zoladz
item.category: You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love (Album)
item.media:credit: Dimitrios Kambouris/Getty Images for American Express
item.media:description: Olivia Rodrigo sings about a crush on “Drop Dead,” the first single from “You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love.”

item.title: ‘Everyone Is Lying to You for Money’ Digs Into Cryptocurrency
item.description: “Everyone Is Lying to You for Money,” directed by Ben McKenzie, gives a solid introduction to virtual currency, and its traps.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson
item.category: Everyone Is Lying to You for Money (Movie)

item.title: The Choreographer Kyle Abraham Embraces the Big Perm and Boombox Era
item.description: His “Cassette Vol. 1” has a 1980s mix tape soundtrack and nods to postmodern American dance vocabulary.
item.dc:creator: Brian Seibert
item.category: Skirball Center for the Performing Arts

item.title: D4vd Arrested in Connection With Death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez
item.description: The singer was detained in connection with the death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, who died before her 15th birthday. D4vd’s lawyers said they would vigorously defend his innocence.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens and Orlando Mayorquín
item.media:credit: Jordan Strauss/Invision, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The body of a teenage girl was found in a car belonging to the singer D4vd.

item.title: Pragmata Is a ‘Sad Dad’ Game Without the Emotional Bond
item.description: Pragmata features a grizzled soldier and a robot child but doesn’t have the emotional bond of The Last of Us or The Walking Dead.
item.dc:creator: Yussef Cole

item.title: Five Free Movies to Stream Now
item.description: This month’s picks make up a quintet of paranoia that speaks to the ethos of unease and dread endemic to the modern moment
item.dc:creator: Brandon Yu
item.media:credit: Columbia Pictures/Filmways Pictures, via Getty Images
item.media:description: John Travolta as a movie sound-effects technician who might have recorded a murder in “Blow Out,” directed by Brian De Palma.

item.title: Kendrick Lamar’s Protégé Baby Keem Tells the Whole Story, Warts and All
item.description: The 25-year-old rapper and producer knows he’s benefited from his cousin’s support. But the path to his autobiographical album, “Casino,” was his alone.
item.dc:creator: Ross Scarano and Clement Pascal
item.media:credit: Clement Pascal for The New York Times
item.media:description: It has taken Baby Keem until now to feel open and skilled enough to pound his own tale of hardship, perseverance and extraordinary opportunity into a pointed piece of art.

item.title: Luke Tennie’s Journey From ‘Shrinking’ to ‘The Pitt’ and Beyond
item.description: After a breakout performance on “Shrinking,” the actor can now be seen on “Abbott Elementary” and “The Pitt.”
item.dc:creator: Alexis Soloski and Victor Llorente
item.media:credit: Victor Llorente for The New York Times

item.title: Lawsuit Against Alec Baldwin for ‘Rust’ Shooting Heads Toward Trial
item.description: Although the criminal case against Mr. Baldwin was thrown out, the actor still faces civil cases about the fatal shooting of a cinematographer in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.media:credit: Angela Weiss/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A judge dismissed a criminal case against Alec Baldwin in 2024 after determining that the prosecution had withheld evidence.

item.title: The Politically Charged Art of Celeste Dupuy-Spencer
item.description: Her vivid paintings examined religious fundamentalism and events like the Jan. 6 riots at the U.S. Capitol. She died this month at 46.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Celeste Dupuy-Spencer, Painter of Hotly Topical Images, Dies at 46
item.description: Her vibrant yet brooding work explored the crises of the Trump era, with paintings depicting the Jan. 6 riots and a mangled Confederate monument.
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams

item.title: An Explosion Rattles a Toronto Neighborhood. A Drake Video Was to Blame.
item.description: A blast on Thursday night turned out to be for a video shoot, but it unnerved residents who had lived through a propane plant explosion in 2008.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:credit: Simone Joyner/Getty Images for ABA
item.media:description: Drake performing in London last year. He posted two images on social media of an explosion that startled residents near Downsview Park in Toronto.

item.title: Kanye West’s Concert in Poland Is Canceled Amid Furor Over Antisemitic Comments
item.description: The announcement came a day after a government official said, “In a country marked by the history of the Holocaust, we cannot pretend that this is just entertainment.”
item.dc:creator: Michaela Towfighi
item.media:credit: Jordan Strauss/Invision, via Associated Press
item.media:description: After Ye performed two nights in Los Angeles on a comeback tour, he has had concerts across Europe canceled.

item.title: 9 Shows Our Theater Critics Are Talking About
item.description: John Lithgow in “Giant,” a triumphant revival of “Death of a Salesman” and vogueing cats at “The Jellicle Ball”: These productions are worth knowing about.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Sherman
item.media:credit: Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
item.media:description: Nathan Lane and Laurie Metcalf in Joe Mantello’s revival of “Death of a Salesman” at the Winter Garden Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: ‘Thrash’ and Other Action Movies to Stream Now
item.description: This month’s picks include killer sharks, unlikely gangsters, and defiant ballerinas.
item.dc:creator: Robert Daniels

item.title: Is the Movie Star Back? Sort Of.
item.description: Box office earnings seem no longer won by name alone. From “Project Hail Mary” to “F1,” celebrities are campaigning overtime on screens large and small to lead to hits.
item.dc:creator: Robert Daniels

item.title: Metropolitan Opera and MoMA Together Put Kahlo at Center Stage
item.description: In an unusual collaboration for the Met, the opera’s set designer has conceived a companion exhibition, mounted at MoMA.
item.dc:creator: Hilarie M. Sheets

item.title: Recalling When Lower Manhattan Was New Amsterdam
item.description: An exhibition at the New York Historical focuses on the city’s 17th-century roots as a Dutch settlement.
item.dc:creator: Megan McCrea
item.media:credit: Shuran Huang for The New York Times
item.media:description: Russell Shorto, the director of the New Amsterdam Project at the New York Historical, walking in Lower Manhattan and explaining the history of the Dutch settlement.

item.title: At the Guggenheim, Pop Art Engages With Art of the Present
item.description: An exhibition explores examples of Pop Art from the 1960s in dialogue with recent acquisitions by contemporary artists.
item.dc:creator: Robin Pogrebin
item.media:credit: Colin Clark for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lauren Hinkson, the Guggenheim Museum curator who organized the show “Guggenheim Pop: 1960 to Now,” with examples of the art in the show.

item.title: 9 New Movies Our Critics Are Talking About This Week
item.description: Whether you’re a casual moviegoer or an avid buff, our reviewers think these films are worth knowing about.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Stephen Colbert Delights in Trump’s ‘Ongoing Papal Feud’
item.description: The host of “The Late Show” recapped what he called “yet another day when the entire world is on edge over President Trump’s senseless and elective war — with the pope.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Pittsburgh’s Children’s Museum Satisfies a Hunger for Eric Carle
item.description: The institution will feature five of the beloved author’s collage-based books in a series of interactive exhibits meant to engage children.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora
item.media:credit: Ross Mantle for The New York Times
item.media:description: Preschoolers from Pittsburgh Public Schools hiding in and weaving through a field of four-foot-tall blades of grass in “The Very Clumsy Click Beetle” section of “Very Eric Carle.”

item.title: A ‘Cabinet of Wonders’ on Show at the American Academy of Arts and Letters
item.description: Current members of the museum have created a show that draws from, and comments on, the institution’s curious collections.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Graeber
item.category: American Academy of Arts and Letters

item.title: In Its ‘Greater New York’ Show, MoMA PS1 Focuses on the Here and Now
item.description: Even as the institution has grown and changed, it has continued to be a launchpad for emerging artists. This spring, it is putting 53 in the spotlight.
item.dc:creator: Leslie Wayne
item.media:credit: Daniel Terna for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mural titled “Greater New York” (2026) greets visitors to the exhibition. It was created by the Cevallos Brothers, who have been making colorful signs and posters for Latin American stores in Queens for decades.

item.title: Coming to Broadway: Molière, ‘The Full Monty’ and a Play About D.J.s
item.description: Roundabout Theater Company, one of the four nonprofits with Broadway houses, plans three Broadway shows next season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.category: The Grief Eater Near North Bender (Play)
item.media:credit: Cj Rivera/Invision, via Associated Press, Eduardo Munoz/Reuters, Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
item.media:description: Ruben Santiago-Hudson and Kara Young, left and center, will star in a new Dominique Morisseau play, while Bill Irwin, right, will star in a revival of “The Imaginary Invalid.”

item.title: What We Lose When Everything Is ‘-Coded’
item.description: On the social internet, our fascination with analyzing the hidden messages in our culture has been flattened into one word.
item.dc:creator: Dan Brooks

item.title: ‘One Flew Over the Cuckoo’s Nest’ Returns With Contemporary Bite
item.description: A new London production highlights the story’s racial element and shows how much has changed since the play’s 1963 premiere.
item.dc:creator: Houman Barekat
item.category: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest (Play)
item.media:credit: Manuel Harlan
item.media:description: Aaron Pierre as Randle McMurphy, front, and the cast of “One Flew Over the Cuckoo’s Nest” at the Old Vic theater in London.

item.title: A Sculptor’s Life, in Constant Motion
item.description: A career-spanning Alexander Calder exhibition in Paris turns the viewer into a collaborator and lifts the soul.
item.dc:creator: Emily LaBarge and Dmitry Kostyukov
item.media:credit: Dmitry Kostyukov for The New York Times; Artworks: 2025 Calder Foundation, New York/ADAGP, Paris, 2026

item.title: A Thomas J Price Bronze Opens Door to London’s V&A East
item.description: Thomas J Price’s bronze figures present anonymous Black people at heroic scale. After an installation in Times Square sparked a furor, his latest work welcomes visitors to a new museum outpost.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Stanford
item.media:credit: Ayesha Kazim for The New York Times
item.media:description: Price’s sculpture “A Place Beyond,” stands outside the new V&A East in London, which opens Saturday.

item.title: ‘Lee Cronin’s The Mummy’ Review: Fresh From the Sarcophagus
item.description: The movie revives one of cinema’s unforgettable monsters with a macabre makeover, but it spins out in the attempt.
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Rapold

item.title: ‘Greater New York’ Brings the Noisy, Messy Vitality of 53 Artists
item.description: The signature survey by MoMA PS 1 of artists living and working in the city highlights those whose talent is often hidden in plain sight.
item.dc:creator: Max Lakin and Vincent Tullo
item.media:credit: Vincent Tullo for The New York Times

item.title: The Phillies Owner and His Wife Collect Art’s Heavy Hitters
item.description: Mets fans, avert your eyes: John Middleton, majority owner of the Phillies, and his wife have a deep bench of American art stars, and they’ve lent them in a dual display for the 250th.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Leigh Brown and Michelle Gustafson
item.category: Pennsylvania Academy of the Fine Arts
item.media:description: Leigh Middleton and her husband, John Middleton, a.k.a. the secretive collector curators called “Mr. Big,” with “Child and Boat” by Edmund Charles Tarbell (1899).

item.title: Kenya Barris on His New Documentary, ‘Jerry West: The Logo’
item.description: A new documentary by the “Black-ish” creator Kenya Barris looks at the legacy of Jerry West, a figure so crucial to the N.B.A.’s history that he’s the league’s logo.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Abrams

item.title: It’s (Always) the Season for Anime
item.description: There’s a new crop four times a year. This spring, the Ghibli-like “Witch Hat Atelier” and the alternate-future samurai saga “Nippon Sangoku” stand out.
item.dc:creator: Mike Hale
item.category: Nippon Sangoku: The Three Nations of the Crimson Sun (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Crunchyroll
item.media:description: “Witch Hat Atelier” is notable for its exploration of a theme with particular resonance in anime: the correlation of magic with the art of drawing.

item.title: ‘Mother Mary’ Review: Anne Hathaway Strikes a Pose
item.description: The actress plays a pop star who reunites with Michaela Coel’s fashion designer. But the spectacle you see onscreen is far more engaging than the dialogue.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: ‘Normal’ Review: This Town Is Anything But
item.description: Bob Odenkirk plays a sheriff who uncovers a dangerous secret in this hyper-violent, small-town crime caper.
item.dc:creator: Jeannette Catsoulis

item.title: ‘Mad Bills to Pay’ Review: Growing Up Unexpectedly
item.description: A girlfriend’s pregnancy upends the life of a young man in the Bronx in this first feature by Joel Alfonso Vargas that unspools with sedulous care.
item.dc:creator: Natalia Winkelman

item.title: ‘Erupcja’ Review: Dancing Through the Ash
item.description: Charli XCX stars in this drama about a young woman who can’t quite tell the difference between freedom and fleeing.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Yu
